evals.expected_output: A search is run against the vault path resolved from $OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH (or a path confirmed with the user), and the assistant reports the filenames of any matching notes.
assertions: The search command (find/grep, or the Grep/Glob tools) targets the vault path resolved from $OBSIDIAN_VAULT_PATH rather than a hardcoded path from a different machine
The response lists the filenames of matching notes found in the vault
If no vault path can be resolved, the assistant asks the user for their vault path instead of guessing or failing silently

evals.prompt: create a note about Chain-of-Thought Prompting and link it to related notes
evals.expected_output: A new note file named in Title Case (e.g. `Chain-of-Thought Prompting.md`) is created in the vault, with a wikilinks section at the bottom linking to related existing notes.
assertions: The created filename uses Title Case
The note content includes a wikilinks section (e.g. a 'Related' heading or a list of `[[...]]` links) at the bottom of the file
The assistant searched for an existing note with the same or a similar title before creating the new one

evals.prompt: create a note about Retrieval Augmented Generation
evals.expected_output: Before creating a new file, the assistant searches the vault for an existing note with the same or a near-matching title (e.g. `RAG.md`, `Retrieval Augmented Generation.md`) and, since one already exists in the vault (see input files), updates or links to it instead of creating a duplicate.
assertions: The assistant runs a search (grep/find, or the Grep/Glob tools) for an existing note with a matching or near-matching title before writing a new file
Because `Retrieval Augmented Generation.md` already exists in the vault, the assistant does not create a duplicate file — it updates or links to the existing note instead
The assistant's response mentions that a matching note already existed
